I cannot quite believe this video is actually produced by BBC. It is not just lopsided praise; it is a dangerously incomplete story. By ignoring the context, the bankruptcies, the colossal waste, and the unsustainable economics of the price war, this video presents a fiction. It mistakes the feverish, chaotic, and wasteful symptoms of a state-driven industrial campaign for a story of organic, sustainable innovation. It celebrates the handful of survivors standing on a mountain of failed companies and wasted capital, and presents it as the future, willfully ignorant of the unsustainable foundation upon which that future is built. The difference between gas cars catching on fire and electric cars, is that electric cars catch fire when they're not being used in your garage, driveway, parked, etc. A gas car won't catch fire if it is been sitting there overnight. It takes heat to ignite the gas. Electric batteries are constantly holding back A LOT of power and once the battery has a fault, it ruptures. Can even be explosive with no warning at all. Electric fires are a lot more violent than gas and take like 10x the amount of time to extinguish.

In my opinion, the electric vehicle market will go no where until there is a standards body that mandates uniformity across all EVs in regards to the batteries and charging. One of the clips said an F150 still has a 36 gallon tank regardless of how old it is and an electric vehicle battery doesn't give out the same amount of stored power as it ages. But it goes further than that. Gas is gas. I can use current gas to run a 1950s car as I can with a current model internal combustion engine car. You cannot say that with EVs. Even within the current manufacturer, there has been instances where you cannot get a replacement battery. I mention the standards body because something needs to be done to make the electrical connecters for both charging and for the battery connections to be uniform. With many battery operated devices around the house, you can have confidence you can get any AA battery to put into the device and it will work. Not with an electric vehicle.
